subject: Biology
title: The Golgi Apparatus
description: A 60-second lesson on The Golgi Apparatus.

hook.text: Camillo Golgi spotted a strange stacked structure in nerve cells in 1898.

buildup.text: The Golgi receives proteins from the ER, modifies them, and sorts them for delivery.

discovery.text: It adds sugar tags and molecular zip codes so each protein finds the right destination.

twist.text: Without the Golgi, proteins pile up with no address. Cells malfunction and diseases follow.

climax.text: Finished products are packed into vesicles and shipped to the membrane or beyond.

punchline.text: The cell's post office: tag it, pack it, ship it.

quiz.question: What does the Golgi apparatus do with proteins?
